name: Catch certificate needed?
pageTitle: Catch certificates - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Documents
heading: Catch certificates
bodyCopy: You must add catch certificates for all fish species unless they are exempt from illegal, unreported and unregulated (IUU) fishing controls.
For help with catch certificates, check the guidance on importing or moving fish into the UK (opens in new tab).
If catch certificates are missing or incomplete, the consignment is at high risk of being rejected.
Make sure you provide all required documents for this notification promptly to avoid disruptions and delays in the clearance process.

structure.text: You must add catch certificates for all fish species unless they are exempt from illegal, unreported and unregulated (IUU) fishing controls.
For help with catch certificates, check the guidance on importing or moving fish into the UK (opens in new tab).

If you do not have catch certificates now -> [expanded] If catch certificates are missing or incomplete, the consignment is at high risk of being rejected. / Make sure you provide all required documents for this notification promptly to avoid disruptions and delays in the clearance process.
Do you need to add catch certificates?
structure.govukComponent: Heading (govuk-heading-l). NOTE: rendered as a sibling immediately ABOVE the <form>, entirely OUTSIDE the fieldset — the fieldset legend is empty (whitespace only). It is NOT wrapped in the legend.

structure.text: Yes / No – all the wild fish in this consignment are exempt from IUU fishing controls

govukComponents.usage: Section caption "Documents" above the page H1 (span id=page-secondary-title)

Second H1 "Do you need to add catch certificates?" acting as the radios question. It sits OUTSIDE the <form>, immediately above it — it is NOT inside the fieldset legend (the legend is empty). So the radio group has no accessible name from its legend.

Paragraph wrapping the "Cancel and return to hub" link. NOTE: the outer grid-row div also carries the govuk-body class (id=catch-certificate-exemption-page), so all page copy inherits govuk-body typography.
Collapsible "If you do not have catch certificates now" disclosure explaining rejection risk of missing/incomplete certificates

Groups the Yes/No radios. The legend (govuk-fieldset__legend) is EMPTY — it contains only whitespace and provides no accessible name. The question copy lives in a detached H1 that sits above the form, outside this fieldset.
The single decision control: do you need to add catch certificates? (Yes/No). data-module=govuk-radios.
govukComponents.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 38; page object CatchCertificateNeededPage.ts:6-8 drives the Yes radio via locator '#catch-certificate-needed'

govukComponents.usage: "Save and return to hub" (id=save-and-return-button) and "Save and continue" (id=button-save-and-continue) — two primary-styled submit buttons, both with data-prevent-double-click=true
External guidance link "importing or moving fish into the UK (opens in new tab)." href=https://www.gov.uk/guidance/importing-or-moving-fish-to-the-uk, target=_blank rel=noopener noreferrer

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Two <h1> elements on one page: "Catch certificates" (govuk-heading-xl) and "Do you need to add catch certificates?" (govuk-heading-l), the second sitting outside the form above the fieldset
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The page carries an introductory H1 ("Catch certificates") with explanatory copy and a Details disclosure, then a SECOND H1 that is the actual radios question. That second H1 is rendered as a plain sibling immediately before the <form> — it is NOT inside the fieldset legend, and the fieldset legend is left empty.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Two problems compound here: (a) GOV.UK guidance is one H1 per page, and this is an intro-plus-question hybrid; (b) the fieldset legend is empty, so the radio group has no programmatic accessible name — the H1 is not associated with the group. This is worse than the recommended legend-as-H1 pattern.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Question page pattern: single H1 rendered INSIDE the fieldset legend (govuk-fieldset__legend--l), with intro copy as govuk-body paragraphs and the disclosure as a Details component beneath. Do not leave the legend empty; do not carry two H1s forward.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 38 (main outerHTML: <h1 class="govuk-heading-l govuk-!-margin-bottom-4"> precedes <form>; <legend class="govuk-fieldset__legend "> contains only whitespace)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom link-styling classes applied to the external guidance link and the Details summary text (non-govuk hover/underline behaviour).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke link styling outside the Design System link component; introduces custom CSS the new app should avoid.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Use the standard govuk-link component and its default styling; do not override underline/hover.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 38 (classes 'link-no-underline link-hover-highlight' on the guidance <a> and on the govuk-details__summary-text span)

fields.name: catch-certificate-needed
fields.label: Do you need to add catch certificates?
options: No – all the wild fish in this consignment are exempt from IUU fishing controls
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 38 — id=catch-certificate-needed value=true (Yes), id=catch-certificate-needed-2 value=false (No). Page object corroborates the Yes radio locator '#catch-certificate-needed' at page-objects/notification/CatchCertificateNeededPage.ts:6-8. The workflow only ever selects Yes: workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:899 (radioCatchCertificateNeeded.check; trace action 38 source = ched-p-workflows.ts:899:66) — the No/exempt branch (value=false, id catch-certificate-needed-2) is never exercised by any test in the corpus.
fields.notes: The label copy above is the detached H1 text, NOT the fieldset legend — the legend is empty, so this control has no programmatic accessible name in the rendered DOM (confirmed, action 38). required=false is the DOM HTML5 attribute only (neither radio carries a `required` attribute); selecting one radio is functionally mandatory to progress via 'Save and continue' but no test asserts the mandatory-selection validation. observedValues holds only 'true' because every driven journey picks Yes. The page object CatchCertificateNeededPage.ts:6-12 exposes ONLY two members — radioCatchCertificateNeeded ('#catch-certificate-needed', the Yes radio) and btnSaveAndContinue (getByRole button 'Save and continue', lines 10-12). It exposes NO locator for the No/exempt radio, the 'Save and return to hub' button, or the 'Cancel and return to hub' link — so all three of those surfaces are untested by the QA corpus, not merely un-picked (inferred, CatchCertificateNeededPage.ts:1-13, read in full and verified).

validationMessages.trigger: "Save and continue" clicked with neither radio selected
validationMessages.evidence: No error-state trace was mined for this page and no test asserts a validation message. The QA page object (CatchCertificateNeededPage.ts) exposes no error-summary locator and the workflow (ched-p-workflows.ts:899-900) always selects Yes before continuing, so the mandatory-selection error was never exercised. Verbatim copy is unknown — this is a question for a human.

openQuestions: The radio inputs carry no `required` attribute in the DOM (HTML5), but selecting one is functionally mandatory to progress. What is the verbatim validation error when neither radio is chosen and "Save and continue" is clicked? No error-state trace was mined and no QA test asserts it — mark gap.

The url slug in the rendered page is .../catch-certificates (confirmed via the returnUrl hidden field /protected/notifications/DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979/catch-certificates and the cancel link /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979/overview), not the field id catch-certificate-needed. Note the internal page div id is catch-certificate-exemption-page. The page-position url pattern in the brief should read /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/{chedReference}/catch-certificates.

The question H1 sits outside the form and the fieldset legend is empty (confirmed, action 38). In the rebuild, decide whether to move the question into the legend (legend-as-H1) so the radio group is properly labelled.

Two submit buttons are present ("Save and return to hub" id=save-and-return-button and "Save and continue" id=button-save-and-continue). No QA test exercises "Save and return to hub" or the "Cancel and return to hub" link (the workflow only clicks "Save and continue", ched-p-workflows.ts:900). Confirm both submit buttons perform a save and differ only in the post-save destination (hub vs next page); the new app should decide whether to keep the dual-button pattern.

The "No – all the wild fish in this consignment are exempt from IUU fishing controls" branch (radio value=false, id catch-certificate-needed-2) is never selected by any QA workflow — every driven journey picks Yes (ched-p-workflows.ts:899) and proceeds into the attach-catch-certificate upload flow (attachCatchCertificate → manageCatchCertificates → addCatchCertificateDetails). The downstream route for No (presumably skipping the upload flow entirely) is uncovered by both traces and tests — confirm the conditional route for No.

Post-Yes flow evidence (inferred, ched-p-workflows.ts:898-928, plus subsequent trace actions 43/47/51 on the same trace: reference field #catch-certificate-reference-1, a 'Flag state of catching vessel(s)' combobox typed 'France', and a 'Do you need to upload more catch certificates?' Yes/No group): selecting Yes leads to an Attach catch certificate page, then a Manage catch certificates page, then an Add catch certificate details page (reference, flag state, etc.). These are separate pages/steps in the IUU document-gathering flow and are NOT part of this page — flagged here only to bound where the Yes branch goes.
